steps.title: Separate the two clauses
steps.content: The first clause says the observatory sites number nearly five dozen. The next clause says the radars track mesospheric winds, so the blank must properly separate the two complete ideas.

steps.title: Avoid the comma splice
steps.content: A, C, and D leave the two independent clauses joined by only a comma or an incomplete modifier. A period followed by "Located across the globe" correctly starts the second sentence and modifies "the radars." The answer is B.
